architectReview.evidence: Architecture: single session-manager owns the browser handle + a FIFO queue over EVERY session-touching call (launch/navigate/snapshot/click/type/scroll/screenshot/teardown), with depth-bounded busy back-pressure and idle teardown routed through the same queue with claim-gating (A4). Tools are factory-wired to one process-wide manager. Reads use native ariaSnapshot({mode:'ai'}); writes resolve ref/selector via snapshot-resolver (C1: bare eXX id, resolver prepends aria-ref=) into engine humanized input. Product: launch forces humanize (no param disables); navigate uses non-throwing classifyTargetUrl and refuses non-ok dispositions with needs-preflight and NO navigation; teardown is claim-gated; snapshot is untrusted-marked/truncated (C2). Code: teardown closes the browser (closes contexts+pages), fixing a process-leak; error-signal maps target-safety throws to needs-preflight so no raw throw escapes.
commands: leader architect review over G003 changeset (session-manager, snapshot-resolver, tools/lifecycle|navigate|interact, error-signal target-safety rule, tests)

executorQa.evidence: 24-test mcp suite green: real-browser humanize-contract proves cloak_click multi-step trusted motion + paced cloak_type and that a synthetic click yields no humanized motion (teeth); navigate refuses unsafe/approval-required with no goto; queue serializes + busy-bounds; teardown + idle respect claim-gating; snapshot untrusted-marked.

artifactRefs.description: Phase 2 API QA report: commands + adversarial cases
browser automation transcript of the engine humanized input path the Phase-2 tools invoke
non-uniform screenshot of the humanized run (visible typed text)

contractCoverage.obligation: cloak_click/cloak_type route through engine humanized input; non-humanized action detectable
unsafe/approval-required URLs refused with no navigation
interleaved calls serialized; overflow -> busy, never a raced page
teardown/idle refuse to drop a session with pending claims unless forced

surfaceEvidence.invocation: direct handler calls (navigate/queue/teardown) + live stdio tools/list of 12 cloak_* tools
launchCloakBrowser + cloak_click/cloak_type through the session manager; motion recorded

adversarialCases.scenario: engine cloak_click vs synthetic DOM click
adversarialCases.expectedBehavior: engine path humanized; synthetic path 0 moves

iteration.evidence: Leader ai-slop-cleaner sweep on the G003 changeset: caught and fixed a real teardown resource leak (was closing context, not browser -> hung process); no fallback masking (needs-preflight/busy/needs-confirmation are grounded states); no duplication (shared TARGET_SCHEMA, single resolver, single manager); no dead code. Full 24-test suite rerun clean after the fix.
